issues: Draft says the final score was 5-3 but then says 'Centennial Cup playoff game' — the score is actually correct at 5-3, but note: this is the Abbott Cup (Western Canada Tier 2 junior final), NOT the Centennial Cup final. The Centennial Cup is the national championship; this series is described in the OCR as the 'Western Canada Tier 2 junior final', which is the Abbott Cup.
The draft describes this as a 'Centennial Cup playoff game' which is inaccurate — the OCR clearly states it is the 'Western Canada Tier 2 junior final' (Abbott Cup).
The draft omits the detail that Hervey was a former member of the Victoria Cougars in major junior hockey, which is a notable contextual fact in the article.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeat Humboldt Broncos 5-3 to take a 3-2 series lead in the Western Canada Tier 2 junior final (Abbott Cup).
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Matt Hervey, a former Victoria Cougars major junior player, scored two goals to lead the Richmond Sockeyes to a 5-3 win over the Humboldt Broncos in a Western Canada Tier 2 junior final (Abbott Cup) playoff game on Sunday night. The victory gave the Sockeyes a 3-2 lead in the best-of-seven series. Game six was scheduled in Richmond on April 28.
review.notes: The draft misidentifies this series as the Centennial Cup, but the OCR clearly labels it the "Western Canada Tier 2 junior final," which corresponds to the Abbott Cup. The Centennial Cup is the subsequent national championship. Score and headline/byline are otherwise correct.
